phase.goal: Real backend serves fixture-backed TodayPayload over real auth + real profile + real access. Frontend leaves NEXT_PUBLIC_USE_FIXTURES=true mode.

wave.title: Project skeleton: alembic, db sessions, FastAPI app shell
wave.write-scope: apps/api/app/core/config.py · apps/api/app/db/session.py · apps/api/alembic.ini · apps/api/alembic/env.py · apps/api/app/main.py · apps/api/pyproject.toml
wave.entry-criteria: artifact pack approved.

exit-criteria.criterion: uvicorn app.main:app starts and /api/health returns 200 with version/git_sha.
alembic upgrade head succeeds against empty DB.

wave.deliverables: healthcheck route · settings via pydantic-settings · DB session helpers

wave.title: Contract boundary: pick source of truth between Pydantic, zod, and packages/contracts
wave.write-scope: packages/contracts/* · scripts/contracts/* · package.json (scripts only) · .github/workflows/contracts.yml · apps/api/app/contracts/* OR apps/api/app/schemas/_base.py (per chosen option) · lib/contracts/* (only if Option B reduces it to forms)
wave.freeze-scope: all feature routes · all UI components · apps/solarsage/* · requirements.xml · technology.xml · knowledge-graph.xml
wave.entry-criteria: W-1.1 closed · controller has recorded a Decision (A/B/C) in grace/packets/W-1.1B.md.

exit-criteria.criterion: Exactly one source of truth is documented in packages/contracts/README.md.
`pnpm contracts:check` (or equivalent) regenerates artifacts deterministically and CI fails on drift.
Frontend smoke on NEXT_PUBLIC_USE_FIXTURES=true renders /day/today byte-identical to pre-wave.
No existing field name or meta.contract_version value changed.

wave.deliverables: generator script · committed generated artifacts · CI step · README stating chosen option · parity test scaffold

wave.title: Observability canon §8: envelope, redaction list, events registry
wave.write-scope: grace/canon/observability.xml · grace/development-plan.xml (insertion of this wave + W-1.6 + W-1.7 only) · grace/packets/W-CANON-LOG.md
wave.freeze-scope: everything under apps/ · everything under lib/ · packages/contracts/* · all UI components · all feature routes · grace/requirements.xml · grace/technology.xml · grace/knowledge-graph.xml
wave.entry-criteria: W-1.1B closed.

exit-criteria.criterion: grace/canon/observability.xml exists and is internally consistent (envelope §8.2, levels §8.3, privacy §8.4, events registry §8.5, sampling §8.6, gates §8.8 all populated).
Redaction list in §8.4 is exhaustive: every PII / secret / payment / telegram / birth-data key from the data model is enumerated with redact-mode and rationale.
Events registry in §8.5 covers every business event referenced anywhere in development-plan.xml at the time of merge. Each event has owner-wave and payload schema.
Reviewer (controller) signs off in grace/packets/W-CANON-LOG.md under "## Decision".
No code changes under apps/ or lib/. CI runs lint-only on this PR.

wave.deliverables: grace/canon/observability.xml · grace/packets/W-CANON-LOG.md · this insertion in development-plan.xml

wave.title: Telegram auth + users + user_profiles
wave.write-scope: apps/api/app/api/auth.py · apps/api/app/services/telegram_auth.py · apps/api/app/api/profile.py · apps/api/app/db/models.py (users, user_profiles) · alembic/versions/0001_users.py

exit-criteria.criterion: POST /api/auth/telegram validates HMAC and upserts users.
GET/PUT /api/profile reads and writes user_profiles.
UC-PROFILE-EDIT invalidation logic stubbed (no caches yet, just marker function).
Tampered initData yields 401 with no DB write.

wave.deliverables: session cookie helper · profile schemas · 4 unit tests for HMAC

wave.title: Pilot: GET /api/day/:date returns fixture-backed TodayPayload
wave.write-scope: apps/api/app/api/day.py · apps/api/app/services/today_service.py · apps/api/app/services/access_service.py (stub returning state=full) · apps/api/app/schemas/today.py · apps/api/app/fixtures/day_*.json
wave.freeze-scope: auth · profile · packages/contracts/today.ts (READ-ONLY reference) · all calendar/checkin/llm code
wave.entry-criteria: W-1.2 merged · TodayPayload contract reviewed against docs/05.

exit-criteria.criterion: GET /api/day/2026-05-24 returns valid TodayPayload (fixture mapped to date).
Response validates against packages/contracts/today.ts via JSON Schema test.
meta.contract_version, meta.cached, meta.generatedAt are present.
p95 latency under 500ms over 100 sequential calls in tests.

wave.deliverables: day endpoint · fixture loader · contract validator · 6 tests including invalid date and not-onboarded.

wave.title: GET /api/calendar?month=YYYY-MM with neutral statuses

exit-criteria.criterion: Returns CalendarPayload for prev/current/next month grid.
days[].access matches access_service stub.
Out-of-range months yield INVALID_DATE error.

wave.title: Frontend leaves fixtures mode and points at real API

exit-criteria.criterion: Setting NEXT_PUBLIC_USE_FIXTURES=false routes traffic to API_BASE.
e2e: open /day/today → renders headline, top flags, reading paragraphs, week strip.
e2e: open /calendar → renders 3-month grid; tap on a day navigates to /day/:date.

wave.demo: End of PHASE-1 product slice: clickable product on real backend with fixture content. Pilot acceptance for UC-DAY-VIEW met. Logging spine still pending — added in W-1.6/W-1.7 before PHASE-2 starts.

wave.title: Logging spine: envelope, correlation, redactor, events codegen, gates 11/12/13
wave.write-scope: apps/api/app/core/logging.py · apps/api/app/core/logging_events.py (codegen output, committed) · apps/api/app/core/redactor.py · apps/api/app/middleware/correlation.py · apps/api/app/main.py (mount middleware + logger init only; no business logic change) · apps/api/app/api/auth.py (replace ad-hoc prints with log.event, no behaviour change) · apps/api/app/api/profile.py (same retrofit) · apps/api/app/api/day.py (same retrofit) · apps/api/app/api/calendar.py (same retrofit) · lib/log/index.ts · lib/log/redactor.ts · lib/log/events.gen.ts (codegen output, committed) · lib/log/correlation.ts · lib/api/* (inject correlation header, no contract change) · scripts/codegen/events_from_canon.py · scripts/codegen/events_from_canon.ts · .github/workflows/observability.yml
wave.freeze-scope: grace/canon/observability.xml (READ-ONLY reference) · packages/contracts/* · all DB models · all UI components · all alembic migrations
wave.entry-criteria: W-CANON-LOG merged. W-1.5 merged (FE on real API). Skeleton of every endpoint that needs instrumentation already exists.

exit-criteria.criterion: Backend logger emits envelope conforming to canon §8.2 on stdout-json (prod-mode) and stderr-pretty (dev-mode).
Frontend logger emits the same envelope to browser console (dev) or buffers in memory (prod, shipping deferred to W-1.7).
Correlation middleware reads/mints X-Correlation-Id and round-trips it on every request. Echoed in response header. Frontend api client injects it on every fetch.
Codegen produces logging_events.py and events.gen.ts byte-identically from observability.xml. CI fails on drift (gate-11).
Redactor canary tests cover every key in §8.4 redact-keys and every pattern in §8.4 redact-patterns. Sentinel never appears in serialized output (gate-12).
Every log line emitted in the test suite parses as JSON and validates against the §8.2 envelope schema (gate-13).
Existing endpoints (auth, profile, day, calendar) emit their declared business events from §8.5 with correct payload shape. No new endpoints created in this wave.
e2e: open /day/today → backend log line carries the same correlation_id as the browser request, asserted in test.

wave.deliverables: backend logger · frontend logger · correlation middleware · shared redactor · events codegen + committed artifacts · gates 11/12/13 in CI · retrofitted call-sites in already-merged endpoints · 1 e2e correlation round-trip test

wave.title: Frontend → backend log shipping via POST /api/_log
wave.write-scope: apps/api/app/api/_log.py · apps/api/app/services/log_intake.py · apps/api/app/core/rate_limit.py (only if not already introduced earlier) · lib/log/shipper.ts · lib/log/index.ts (wire shipper behind GRACE_LOG_SHIPPING flag) · .env.example
wave.freeze-scope: grace/canon/observability.xml · core logging envelope from W-1.6 · auth · profile · day/calendar services · all DB models
wave.entry-criteria: W-1.6 merged. Gates 11/12/13 stable in CI for at least one PR cycle.

exit-criteria.criterion: POST /api/_log accepts a batch of envelopes, validates each against §8.2, runs the same backend redactor, and forwards to stdout-json. No log_intake DB table is added unless controller explicitly decides otherwise in the packet.
Frontend shipper batches by 50 envelopes or 5s, whichever first, with backoff on 429/5xx and a hard kill-switch on GRACE_LOG_SHIPPING=off (default off in dev, on in prod).
Endpoint enforces auth (session cookie required) and per-user rate limit by user_id_hash from §8.4.
Endpoint rejects malformed envelopes with 400 and emits log.intake_rejected (warn) with reject reason.
e2e: a click in the web app produces a log line on the backend with the same correlation_id within 10s.
Load test: 1k envelopes/min sustained for 5 min without backend OOM and without dropping more than 0.1% (drops counted via log.intake_dropped).

wave.deliverables: shipping endpoint · client batcher with kill-switch · rate limiter · 6 tests (malformed, oversized, kill-switch, auth-required, rate-limited, happy-path) · 1 load-test fixture

phase.goal: Port the legacy frontend (legacy/frontend/**) into the active tree under app/(grace)/** and components/** with marker discipline, contract-only typing, and lint/marker gates. Keep legacy/** read-only and unimported (INV-LEGACY-INERT). Source of truth for each wave is its packet under grace/packets/W-2.X.md.

wave.title: Frontend GRACE conformance: lint/marker gates + 4 negative tests
wave.entry-criteria: W-1.1B closed.

wave.title: Frontend types reconciliation (additive Pydantic, no UI)
wave.entry-criteria: W-2.0 merged.

wave.title: Shell + Today migration (first UI inside GRACE perimeter)
wave.entry-criteria: W-2.1 merged · W-1.3 merged (real /api/day available for follow-up wiring) OR fixture mode active per packet decision.

wave.title: Calendar + Day pages (first multi-route + dynamic segment)
wave.entry-criteria: W-2.2 merged · W-1.4 merged OR fixture mode active per packet decision.

wave.title: Chat: screen + hook + reducer (frontend port on fixtures)
wave.entry-criteria: W-2.3 merged · W-CHAT-INTAKE closed (Decision A or B).
wave.note: Backend for chat (POST /api/chat/messages, storage, rate-limit, real streaming) is intentionally out of scope here. It is owned by W-CHAT-1. chat.* events stay reserved (emitted=false) until W-CHAT-3.

wave.title: Readings: general + natal + widgets (block-renderer fan-out)
wave.entry-criteria: W-2.4 merged.

wave.title: Profile + Paywall + Trial banner + Telegram-init
wave.entry-criteria: W-2.5 merged.
wave.note: Trial banner is wired to a real useAccess() in this wave (replaces the W-2.2 stub). Backend access service is still fixture-backed; the real access state machine lands in PHASE-ACCESS.

wave.title: Onboarding flow (5-step state machine)
wave.entry-criteria: W-2.6 merged.
wave.note: Reduces the W-2.6 /onboarding-placeholder into a real /onboarding route. Reducer + steps + cities lookup; persistence side-effect at submit writes the onboarded flag consumed by useOnboarded() from W-2.6.

wave.title: JS test gate (vitest for lib/grace/** reducers)
wave.entry-criteria: W-2.7 merged.
wave.note: Symmetric to the W-1.1 pytest gate. Lights up vitest and turns the previously-inert *.test.ts files (chat reducer from W-2.4, onboarding reducer from W-2.7) into a real CI gate. No feature module.

phase.goal: Real access state replaces stub; referral grants 14 days to both; locked days show preview + soft lock. Renamed from former PHASE-2-ACCESS to free the W-2.x namespace for the frontend port phase.

wave.title: access_ledger, access_service.can_access_day
wave.entry-criteria: W-1.5 merged.

exit-criteria.criterion: For 14d referral_bonus → days 0..13 full, day 14 preview.
Subscription consumed only after bonus.
10 unit tests covering boundary days.

wave.title: Referral codes + signup hook
wave.entry-criteria: W-ACCESS.1 merged.

exit-criteria.criterion: New invitee signup writes 2 ledger rows of 14 days.
Repeat invite same user → idempotent.

wave.title: Preview/locked TodayPayload + soft lock UI
wave.entry-criteria: W-ACCESS.2 merged.

exit-criteria.criterion: Locked day route renders preview payload with subscribe and invite CTAs.
Calendar shows locked badges per access state.

phase.goal: Rewrite SolarSage CLI as HTTP sidecar per docs/11. Backend becomes its only client.

wave.title: Reference collector boundary and parity fixture freeze
wave.freeze-scope: collector implementation unless fixing reproducibility defects found by fixture generation

exit-criteria.criterion: Existing deep collector is declared as reference-only, not a product runtime.
Golden JSON fixtures exist for controller-approved charts and target dates.
Future sidecar/split work has a parity gate against the golden fixtures.

wave.title: Sidecar scaffolding (FastAPI app, /v1/health, settings, ephemerides path)
wave.freeze-scope: backend services that don't yet call sidecar

exit-criteria.criterion: uvicorn solarsage.app:app on 127.0.0.1:18091 returns /v1/health 200.

wave.title: /v1/natal endpoint + parity test against legacy CLI on sample_params.json

exit-criteria.criterion: For sample_params.json (Vasiliy), planets longitudes match legacy within 1e-6 deg.
calculation_version is bumped and recorded in response meta.

wave.title: /v1/transits and /v1/range

exit-criteria.criterion: Range endpoint computes daily transits over a window without timeouts (p95 < 3s for 31-day range).

wave.title: solarsage_client + natal/period/daily snapshot caches

exit-criteria.criterion: Repeated /api/day/:date hits use natal cache; cold→warm latency drops > 80%.
Birth-data edit invalidates all snapshots for the user.

phase.goal: Real AstroSignal[] + SemanticLayer; dayStatus computed by backend.

wave.title: Scoring canon data schemas and v1 YAML

exit-criteria.criterion: Canon YAML validates against schemas and includes a version field.
No scoring consumer can start if required canon files are missing or invalid.
Static check prevents new hardcoded sphere tables, dignity constants, aspect thresholds, and top-N aspect cutoffs outside canon data.

wave.title: Normalization to AstroSignal[]
wave.exit-criteria: For sample_params.json on 2026-05-24, AstroSignal output matches snapshot fixture committed by the team lead.

wave.title: Activation layer, convergence bonus, scoring, dayQuality, dayStatus, top flags

exit-criteria.criterion: Activation layer folds profection, firdaria, solar return, solar arc, progressions, and target-date transits into per-sphere evidence.
Convergence bonus is additive and requires at least two independent techniques pointing to the same planet/house/sphere.
Status mapping matches docs/04 §11 table.
flags count constraint (2..4) enforced.

wave.title: SemanticLayer assembly + cache
wave.exit-criteria: semantic_layers row written; today_service builds payload from semantic + microcopy stub.

phase.goal: Real "why for me" text via LLM, strict JSON, prompt_version recorded.

wave.title: Prompt v1 + curated context builder + strict JSON validator

exit-criteria.criterion: LLM output validates against C-LLM-RESPONSE.
Output never references internal scoring fields (regex check on banned tokens).

wave.title: today_payloads cache + meta.cached
wave.exit-criteria: Repeat call to /api/day/:date returns cached payload without LLM call.

phase.goal: Telegram payments, subscription state, enforcement of consumption order.

wave.title: Provider integration + webhook
Subscription ledger entries + enforcement

phase.goal: Natal reading rendered via versioned blocks.

wave.title: Backend: NatalReadingPayload (sections × blocks)
Frontend: block renderer + overview/section routes

phase.goal: Evening checkin and morning yesterdayEcho closure (docs/13).

wave.title: evening_checkins table + POST/GET endpoints
yesterday_service.build_closure + composer integration
Worker: 20:00 push notifications by user TZ

phase.goal: microcopy.v1.json with ≥70 entries, validation in CI, miss tracking.

wave.title: Schema, dictionary v1, services/microcopy.py
microcopy_misses table + weekly report

phase.goal: Executable verification matrix: unit/integration/e2e tests with visual regression. Auth-first approach for all endpoint/e2e tests.

wave.title: Backend integration tests with authenticated_client fixture
wave.entry-criteria: W-1.3 merged (day endpoint) · W-1.4 merged (calendar endpoint) · W-ACCESS.1 merged (access service)

exit-criteria.criterion: UC-DAY-VIEW scenarios S1-S5 covered: valid auth → TodayPayload, not onboarded → 422, no access → preview, invalid date → 400, cold call → full pipeline.
UC-CAL-NAV scenarios S1-S3 covered: valid month → 3-month grid, out of range → 400, tap day → navigates.
UC-ACCESS-CHECK scenarios S1-S3 covered: 14d referral → days 0..13 full, referral + subscription → consumption order, no entries → preview.
authenticated_client fixture: login → onboarding → returns client with session cookie.
Cache assertions: meta.cached=false on first call, true on second.
Performance: p95 < 500ms for cached /api/day/:date.

wave.deliverables: test_day_endpoints.py · test_calendar_endpoints.py · test_access_service.py · authenticated_client fixture · pytest passing in CI

wave.title: Frontend unit tests (vitest for hooks/reducers)
wave.entry-criteria: W-2.6 merged (useAccess hook) · W-2.7 merged (onboarding reducer) · W-2.4 merged (chat reducer)

exit-criteria.criterion: useAccess.test.ts: covers referral days countdown, subscription active, expired access, locked state.
chatReducer.test.ts: covers message append, thread switch, loading states.
onboardingReducer.test.ts: covers step navigation, validation, submit.
Coverage 60%+ for lib/grace/** (hooks, reducers, utils).
CI gate: vitest runs in GitHub Actions, fails on test failure.

wave.deliverables: vitest.config.ts · 4+ unit test files · vitest CI gate

wave.title: E2E tests (Playwright) + Visual regression (baseline screenshots)
wave.write-scope: apps/web/playwright.config.ts · apps/web/e2e/helpers/auth.ts (authenticateAndOnboard, generateFakeInitData) · apps/web/e2e/auth.spec.ts · apps/web/e2e/day-view.spec.ts · apps/web/e2e/calendar.spec.ts · apps/web/e2e/onboarding.spec.ts · apps/web/e2e/locked-day.spec.ts · apps/web/e2e/visual/today-screen.spec.ts · apps/web/e2e/visual/calendar.spec.ts · apps/web/e2e/visual/__screenshots__/*.png (baseline)
wave.entry-criteria: W-2.0..W-2.7 merged (frontend port complete) · W-TEST-1 merged (backend endpoints tested)

exit-criteria.criterion: UC-TG-AUTH: valid initData → session set, tampered hash → 401.
UC-DAY-VIEW: renders headline, flags, reading; cache hit on second load.
UC-DAY-NAV: tap arrow → URL updates, headline changes, week strip active marker moves.
UC-CAL-NAV: tap day → navigates to /day/:date.
UC-LOCKED-DAY: no access → preview + soft lock + CTAs.
Visual regression: baseline screenshots for today-supportive.png, today-tense.png, locked-preview.png, calendar-3months.png, onboarding-step*.png.
authenticateAndOnboard helper: inject Telegram WebApp API → auto auth → onboarding → /day/today.
CI gate: Playwright runs in GitHub Actions, fails on test failure or visual diff > 0.1%.

wave.deliverables: playwright.config.ts · auth helper · 5+ e2e spec files · baseline screenshots (~5-10MB) · Playwright CI gate

wave.title: CI integration: test workflow, visual diff artifacts, contracts gate
wave.entry-criteria: W-TEST-1 merged · W-TEST-2 merged · W-TEST-3 merged

exit-criteria.criterion: GitHub Actions workflow runs: pytest → vitest → Playwright → contracts:check.
Visual diff artifacts uploaded on Playwright failure.
PR blocked on any test failure.
Performance monitoring: p95 latency for /api/day/:date logged in CI.
contracts:check gate: fails on drift between Pydantic and TypeScript.

wave.deliverables: .github/workflows/test.yml · visual diff upload script · CI gates active

phase.goal: Stand up the real chat backend (endpoint, storage, rate-limit, AI provider) and flip chat.* events from reserved to emitted. The frontend port itself is owned by W-2.4 in PHASE-2-FRONTEND-PORT — this phase covers everything that W-2.4 does NOT do (fixture-only there).
phase.intake: W-CHAT-INTAKE is the only wave allowed to land before Phase-1 closes. It is spec-only: it inventories the legacy module, drafts the Pydantic contract, reserves chat.* events in §8.5 with emitted=false, and records a Decision (A port-1:1 / B rewrite-on-AI-SDK / C drop). All other PHASE-CHAT waves stay frozen until that Decision is recorded.

wave.title: Chat intake: inventory, draft contract, reserve events, decision A/B/C
wave.write-scope: grace/packets/W-CHAT-INTAKE.md · grace/canon/observability.xml (only the §8.5 reserved block "chat.*" with emitted=false) · grace/development-plan.xml (only PHASE-CHAT block + drift rule about legacy chat)
wave.freeze-scope: everything under apps/ · everything under lib/ · packages/contracts/* · legacy/** (read-only inventory only) · all UI components · all feature routes · grace/requirements.xml · grace/technology.xml · grace/knowledge-graph.xml
wave.entry-criteria: W-1.1B closed.

exit-criteria.criterion: grace/packets/W-CHAT-INTAKE.md lists every legacy chat file with line count and one-line role.
Draft Pydantic contract for ChatMessage / ChatHistory / ChatContext is recorded in the packet (not yet under apps/api/app/contracts/).

Events chat.message.sent · chat.message.received · chat.stream.started · chat.stream.finished · chat.stream.failed · chat.rate_limited · chat.tokens.spent are reserved in §8.5 with emitted=false and owner-wave="W-CHAT-3".
Controller records a Decision (A port-1:1 / B rewrite-on-AI-SDK / C drop) under "## Decision".
No code changes under apps/, lib/, app/, components/. CI runs lint-only on this PR.

wave.deliverables: grace/packets/W-CHAT-INTAKE.md · §8.5 reserved-events block · this PHASE-CHAT block

wave.title: Backend: chat contract, threads/messages storage, rate limit, auth-bound endpoints
wave.write-scope: apps/api/app/api/chat.py · apps/api/app/services/chat_service.py · apps/api/app/schemas/chat.py · apps/api/app/db/models.py (chat_threads, chat_messages) · alembic/versions/00XX_chat.py · packages/contracts/* (regenerated)
wave.freeze-scope: app/(grace)/chat/** (owned by W-2.4) · legacy/** · day/calendar/access services
wave.entry-criteria: W-CHAT-INTAKE closed with Decision A or B · W-1.2 merged · W-1.6 merged.

exit-criteria.criterion: POST /api/chat/messages requires session cookie from W-1.2; tampered or missing session yields 401 with no DB write.
Streaming response shape matches the contract drafted in W-CHAT-INTAKE; non-streaming fallback documented.
Per-user rate limit enforced; rate-limited request emits chat.rate_limited and returns 429.
contracts:check passes; meta.contract_version bumped only if shape changed beyond the reserved draft.
W-2.4's frontend chat module can switch its lib/api/chat.ts from fixture to real backend by env flag, with no contract change in the same PR.

wave.deliverables: chat schemas · chat service · alembic migration · contract regeneration · unit tests for HMAC-bound auth on chat endpoints · feature flag for fixture↔real toggle in W-2.4 client

wave.title: Observability: flip chat.* events from reserved to emitted; gates 11/12/13 cover chat
wave.write-scope: grace/canon/observability.xml (§8.5 chat.* emitted=true) · apps/api/app/api/chat.py (log.event call sites) · lib/api/chat.ts (log.event call sites) · tests/log/* · __tests__/log/*
wave.entry-criteria: W-CHAT-1 merged · W-2.4 merged · W-1.7 merged.

exit-criteria.criterion: Every chat.* event in §8.5 is asserted in at least one test on the side that emits it.
Gate-11 (events registry) passes with chat.* now emitted=true.
Redactor canary on chat payloads strips message content fields by policy from §8.4.
One real chat round-trip in e2e produces ≥1 backend log line and ≥1 frontend log line sharing the same request_id.

wave.deliverables: event emissions on both sides · redactor coverage for message content · e2e correlation test

wave.title: Quotas / billing for chat (only if Decision touches monetization)
wave.entry-criteria: W-CHAT-3 merged · PHASE-6 reached the wave that owns access_state for non-day features.

exit-criteria.criterion: Quota source defined (free tier vs subscription) and enforced server-side.
chat.tokens.spent emits actual spend; chat.rate_limited covers quota exhaustion path.

wave.title: GRACE-ready orchestrator runtime adapter and validator
wave.rationale: Before running nonstop agents on this rewrite, the project needs a portable adapter that makes packets, roles, verification profiles, evidence, and Prefect runtime bindings machine-readable.
wave.entry-criteria: Root GRACE artifacts exist and guardrails are runnable locally.

exit-criteria.criterion: grace/orchestrator/project.yml defines repo paths, runtime state, agent defaults, Prefect queues, and evidence policy.
grace/orchestrator/packet.schema.json defines the required machine contract for new packets.
grace/orchestrator/verification_profiles.yml maps verification profile names to portable guardrails commands.
Role contracts exist for architect, planner, coder, verifier, reviewer, and orchestrator.
pnpm guardrails:orchestrator passes and is included in pnpm guardrails:full.

wave.write-scope: grace/orchestrator/** · scripts/check_orchestrator_contracts.py · scripts/test_orchestrator_contracts.py · scripts/guardrails.sh · package.json · grace/*.xml · grace/verification-matrix.md

wave.title: Re-derive authoritative deployment surface (backend + frontend)
wave.rationale: The pre-W-1.0 deployment artifacts (docs/DEPLOY.md, scripts/deploy.sh) reference infra that no longer matches the canon: SolarSage sidecar, /opt/solarsage-astro VDS layout, systemd units astro-api/astro-worker/astro-web, apps/web/ path, and SOLARSAGE_* env-vars without canonical prefix. Both files are marked stale and guarded; they MUST NOT be used as runbooks until this wave defines the real surface.
wave.entry-criteria: W-1.5 merged · canonical env-var prefix fixed · backend deployment target (Vercel functions vs separate host) decided.

exit-criteria.criterion: docs/DEPLOY.md replaced (or superseded by a new doc) with a runbook that matches the canon and drops the stale-header.
scripts/deploy.sh replaced or removed; GRACE_DEPLOY_ALLOW_STALE guard no longer needed.
Env-var canon documented in one place; no SOLARSAGE_* without canonical prefix remains in the non-legacy tree.

wave.title: Split reference collector into product SolarSage service modules
wave.rationale: The current deep collector is valuable as coverage/reference, but product runtime needs a service split: client, raw collectors, derived calculations, scoring, special points, JSON writer, and cache-friendly API surfaces.
wave.entry-criteria: W-3.0 parity fixtures merged · W-4.0 scoring canon data merged

exit-criteria.criterion: Collector logic is split into modules with file/function size guardrails respected.
/natal, /period, /day or equivalent service entrypoints reuse the same calculation core.
Golden fixtures show no unapproved calculation drift from the reference collector.

dep.note: frontend port can run in parallel with PHASE-1 backend waves once W-1.1B closed; individual waves declare per-wave dependencies on W-1.x
normalization/scoring consumers must not hardcode scoring knowledge before canon YAML exists
activation and scoring read canon YAML, not local constants
e2e tests assume backend endpoints work
CI workflow needs all test files
chat events ride on the same logging spine; must not predate W-1.6 merge
chat frontend port lives in W-2.4; observability flip waits for it

drift-protection.rule: Each wave merges as one PR with the wave id in the title.
PR checklist must mention which artifact-pack file changed (if any).
If implementation requires changing requirements.xml or technology.xml, the change is a separate prior PR.
Never edit two waves in the same PR.
If a wave's exit criteria cannot be met, raise a question, do not silently expand scope.

Any change to grace/canon/observability.xml (envelope, levels, redact-list, events registry, sampling, gates) is a separate spec-only PR titled W-CANON-LOG-EXT-N and must merge before any wave that depends on the new shape. Workers MUST NOT add a business event by editing only their own packet — the registry in §8.5 is the closed source of truth and is enforced by gate-11.

Once W-1.6 is merged, every subsequent wave that adds endpoints, workers, or UI flows MUST list the business events it emits (from §8.5) in its packet under "Business events emitted" and assert them in tests. Gate-11 will fail PRs that emit unregistered events.

The legacy AI assistant chat under legacy/frontend/app/(app)/chat, legacy/frontend/components/chat/**, legacy/frontend/hooks/use-chat.ts, legacy/frontend/lib/api/chat.{ts,fixtures.ts}, legacy/frontend/lib/contracts/chat.ts, legacy/frontend/lib/reducers/chat-reducer.ts, legacy/frontend/lib/mocks/chat.ts, legacy/frontend/lib/chat.ts is split across two owners: the frontend port (screen, hook, reducer, fixture-backed lib/api/chat.ts) is owned by W-2.4 in PHASE-2-FRONTEND-PORT, and the backend (POST /api/chat/messages, storage, rate-limit, real streaming) is owned by W-CHAT-1 in PHASE-CHAT. No other wave may add chat files to the active tree. The chat.* event namespace in §8.5 is reserved with emitted=false until W-CHAT-3; emitting any chat.* event before then fails gate-11. W-2.4 ships with chat.* events still reserved (its lib/api/chat.ts uses fixtures and does not emit chat.* events).
